meaning: adj.相当大(或多)的,值得考虑的,相当可观的
examples: Damage to the building was considerable.
She argued her case with considerable passion.
The argument seemed to have considerable merit.
Getting such a loan, for one, is tough unless you have considerable equity in your home.
In the inflationary blowup of the late 1970s, diamonds were the subject of considerable investment fervor.
